declared.title: State of Internet Freedom in Africa 2025: Navigating the Implications of AI on Digital Democracy in Ethiopia
declared.selection_rationale: Single-country companion report to the continent-wide 2025 SIFA report already in this corpus (cipesa-africa-2025-sifa-ai) -- not a duplicate. Confirmed by downloading and extracting the full PDF (28 pages, 9,417 words, all pages text-bearing, no abridgment language found): by Abel Wabella, published September 2025. Coverage-contribution value: specific, dated findings absent elsewhere in this corpus's existing Ethiopia entries (Freedom House FOTN chapters, OONI's 2023 social-media-blocking document) -- the July 2024 deregistration of 1,514 CSOs; Ethiopia's National AI Policy (approved 27 June 2024) identifying defence/security as a priority sector with no ethical standards or oversight, paired with a documented reliance on Pegasus spyware and Chinese facial recognition tech per Citizen Lab's reporting; AI-assisted drone warfare sourcing (Mohajer-6, TB2, Wing Loong) used in the Tigray conflict per the European Council on Foreign Relations' "Deadly Skies" report; and the Personal Data Protection Proclamation No. 1321/2024 relevant to the Fayda digital ID system. Somewhat shorter than the Kenya/Uganda companions but still substantially deeper than the continent report's compressed per-country treatment.
